prose: Frontier-orbital energies of ethylene computed at DFT tier 'standard' = B3LYP/def2-TZVP (density-fit RI) with PySCF, gas phase, charge 0, multiplicity 1. Single-point calculation on the user-supplied geometry (/home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/frontier-orbitals-validation/ethylene/ethylene.xyz); geometry was not re-optimized at this level. SCF converged in 6 cycles. No warnings reported by the engine. Integrity check: trustworthy = true, all checks passed. HOMO = −7.6155818 eV (orbital index 7), LUMO = −0.17986527 eV (orbital index 8), HOMO–LUMO gap = 7.4357165 eV. These are Koopmans-style orbital-energy differences, not excitation energies. Neighbouring orbitals: HOMO−2 = −11.4875 eV, HOMO−1 = −9.8839 eV, LUMO+1 = +1.7201 eV, LUMO+2 = +2.3802 eV. Koopmans descriptors: vertical IP = 7.6156 eV, vertical EA = 0.1799 eV, electronegativity = 3.8977 eV, chemical hardness = 3.7179 eV, electrophilicity index = 2.0431 eV. Total electronic energy = −78.623486 Hartree.
